annotations.text: User sends a request from the CLI: "Write three haiku about Rust programming, then pick the best one."
TUI opens. The coordinator creates the task graph — write-three-haiku fans out to three parallel writers, then pick-the-best gathers results.
Haiku-writing agents spawn in parallel. Three independent agents each compose a haiku about Rust.
Writing completes. Evaluation kicks in — the system scores each haiku before passing results to the judge.
The judge agent reads all three haiku and deliberates. It compares style, adherence to 5-7-5, and Rust relevance.
Judge picks the winner. All tasks complete — scatter-gather pattern finished in under a minute of wall time.
